message.content:
{
  "lecture_id": "probability_001",
  "knowledge_objects": [
    {
      "id": "random_variable",
      "name": "Random Variable",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "A function that assigns a numerical value to each outcome of a random experiment.",
      "source_span": "A random variable is a function that assigns a numerical value to each outcome of a random experiment."
    },
    {
      "id": "probability_distribution",
      "name": "Probability Distribution",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Describes how probability is assigned to the possible values of a random variable.",
      "source_span": "The probability distribution of a random variable describes how probability is assigned to its possible values."
    },
    {
      "id": "expected_value",
      "name": "Expected Value",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": ["mean"],
      "short_definition": "A weighted average of the possible values of a random variable.",
      "source_span": "The expected value of a random variable is a weighted average of its possible values."
    },
    {
      "id": "expected_value_formula",
      "name": "Expected Value Formula",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Formula for the expected value of a discrete random variable: sum of x times P(X=x).",
      "source_span": "\\mathbb{E}[X] = \\sum_x x P(X=x)."
    },
    {
      "id": "variance",
      "name": "Variance",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Measures how far a random variable typically is from its expected value.",
      "source_span": "Variance measures how far a random variable typically is from its expected value."
    },
    {
      "id": "variance_formula",
      "name": "Variance Formula",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Formula for variance: expected squared deviation from the mean.",
      "source_span": "\\operatorname{Var}(X) = \\mathbb{E}[(X-\\mathbb{E}[X])^2]."
    },
    {
      "id": "conditional_probability",
      "name": "Conditional Probability",
      "type": "Concept",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Probability of an event given that another event has occurred.",
      "source_span": "Conditional probability measures the probability of an event given that another event has occurred."
    },
    {
      "id": "conditional_probability_formula",
      "name": "Conditional Probability Formula",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": [],
      "short_definition": "Formula for conditional probability: P(A|B) = P(A∩B)/P(B).",
      "source_span": "P(A \\mid B) = \\frac{P(A \\cap B)}{P(B)}."
    },
    {
      "id": "bayes_rule",
      "name": "Bayes' Rule",
      "type": "Formula",
      "aliases": ["Bayes' theorem"],
      "short_definition": "Rewrites conditional probability in a form useful for updating beliefs.",
      "source_span": "P(A \\mid B) = \\frac{P(B \\mid A)P(A)}{P(B)}."
    }
  ]
}
